content: Because they also said it is their way to give rewards in bigger bulks without abruptly breaking their economy, it should allow future packs to get better/cheaper value, of course said possibility must be seen actually practiced first, the concept of degrading saved pulls to stop account resells and the massive wait times this stagnant accounts  goes through sounds sound on paper, but its only really issue was that it steps on actual players saving on a game that is dupe heavy and is expected to e3 minimum a new fav character, now that it is less invasive and a system that players can actually choose whether to get/receieve or not means there should be little qualms about its existence now.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
Mate, no one complained about how this are now, they complained about how they were when they were changing event rewards to temporary too. As Angbak said, we'll see when they add said bundles.

It still doesn't add up, cause those botting and selling accounts don't buy bundles, so they can give better bundles without changing them to temporary anyway. To me it sounds like they want to turn the preorders to temporary pulls because that's what everyone recommends as a must buy, but right now it's speculation until we see the next preorder.

The other guy is shaming other players for complaining when they wanted to change the event rewards to temporary, which, as you said, would have been a major blow considering this game is very dupe reliant. That is not cool.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content: ? All this issues had been rollbacked, of course no one is complaining about the now because it is their answer to the agressive feedback from BEFORE, they listened and had heard it would be very disatisfactory, this way they are still able to use the resources they did by creating Ethereals , it does not matter if it adds up to your head or not because that is what *they* thought was the answer to *their* issue that *they* saw as troublesome for *them* in the long run, now that they are here, it is time to wait and see how they set it up in practice, they *can* change the preorder and other high value packs to ethereals, but only if it did not create a noticable bulk (15+ increase in cores personally) then can the community once again aggressively remind the company of what they said before, and to actually stay true to their promise, or actively decline their playerbase as punishnent for their consistent lying.

content:
To me fighting grey market is a huge waste of time and resources, and seems to fall in the same fallacy as piracy that corporations keep falling into and never learns because their shareholders are crying FOMO.

Better yet, if there are people willing to pay for a good starting account, then take inspiration and learn from the grey market and offer a similar one-time bundle and move on, rather than make everyone's life worse.

content:
We can start from, arcane dominion sweeping. Gamemode one time achievements. All rewards not being tied to leaderboard, now we get more from leaderboards, if one decides to bother. The entire skin gacha talk, and the 1 time we had the skin gacha the rewards being reasonable.

They still continue to upkeep the core gameplay well, characters of varied playstyles, even simpler ones. 

These are the more recent/non throwaway answers I have. But if we take everything that light had spoken about early, From VA's to QOL stuff. To even simplifying the unit design akin to the JP version. It also all adds up. 

As i've said here at the start. Taking every word from the director who says and changes his mind repeatedly, is dumb. For a lack of a better word. He keeps doing it unprofessionally... yes. But at the same time, IT happens over and over. The only one who can change is you.

Not adding to the once again. THE LIMITED CORES LASTING 3 MONTHS.
thread_title: [Producer's Letter] We Will Not Fail You

content:
Maybe I'm in a strange position.

Cause I think the current state of the game is underwhelming in comparison to year one. Wich had fully fleshed out events with mechanics, enemies and Multiple CG's. What the story was. The boss design actually playing around mechanics and not only the damage numbers/not being  obnoxious long lead up to a mechanical gotcha moment.

I inevitably, sitting with all characters I want, at dupe levels I want, and still having more pulls, while having spent barely anything playing the game since 2014. I'd do a lot to get the game to the state of quality that made me fall for it in the first place. And inevitably, for the Devs to even have the budget to work on the content they promised

Inevitably playing this game for so long. Seeing how the changes play out. Seeing the Devs listen to not inflating the A5 hp anymore... I legitimately, cannot see and don't believe the Devs have a reason to make an under thought bad change that would "ruin the game" and be worth quitting over. (This is in regards to the AA)
